content.full_text:
February 9, 1983

TO: File
FROM: Bob Leidigh
RE: T-83-030

Question:

Marshal Grossman is a member of the Coastal Commission. He is attorney with 10% interest in law firm. He is representing (for a set fee) Mr. Stanfield who is ex-president of 20th Century Fox. Suing for contract action. Seeking injunction freezing all assets of 20th Century Fox -- this includes all assets of a subsidiary, Pebble Beach Corp. (PBC). PBC owns much of land in Del Monte Forest in Monterey. Local Coastal Program (LCP) is prepared by Monterey County. It is being submitted to Coastal Commission for adoption or rejection on 2/10/83. If adopted, then permit authority will shift from Coastal Commission to Monterey County. If rejected, then permit authority will remain with Coastal Commission until new LCP proposal is prepared and adopted. PBC will be greatly affected by these actions as LCP will have major impact upon use (density, development, public access, open space, etc.) to which its land may be put. PBC opposing LCP because it puts too many restrictions on use of land. Given that Stanfield is source of income to Grossman, does Grossman have conflict of interest?

Advice:

Discussions with Ted, Stern (Barbara, preliminarily) -- Nexus test in 18702(b)(3)(B) is met. The objectives of the suit are very similar to issues affected by the governmental decision. There is a substantial similarity in purpose. Decision will affect both the value and the transferability of the assets of 20th Century Fox.

REL: km
